sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: After lobectomy for differentiated thyroid carcinoma, disease monitoring includes physical examination, TSH measurement, and neck ultrasound at 6-12 months. A one-time Tg and TgAb measurement may be considered. After total thyroidectomy without RAI, monitoring includes physical examination, TSH, Tg and TgAb measurement at 6-12 weeks, and neck ultrasound at 6-12 months. After total thyroidectomy with RAI, monitoring includes physical examination, neck ultrasound at 6-12 months, TSH, and Tg and TgAb measurement. If NED, physical examination, TSH, annual Tg and TgAb measurement if stable, and neck ultrasound every 1-3 years for 5-8 years, then discontinue. After 10-15 years of no recurrence for patients at low risk, no further monitoring for thyroid cancer is indicated. For medullary carcinoma, postoperative calcitonin and CEA should be measured 2-3 months postoperatively. Serum Tg determinations, neck ultrasound, and whole body iodine-131 imaging detect recurrent or residual disease in most patients who have undergone total thyroid ablation. Tg measurement is the best means of detecting thyroid tissue, including carcinoma. About 85% of patients are considered to be low risk after surgery for papillary thyroid carcinoma. About 75% of patients with differentiated thyroid carcinoma show tumor recurrences during the first 5 years following treatment, with the remaining recurrences occurring within 8 years after treatment. Nonpalpable, small-volume lymph node recurrences often show little evidence of disease progression over many years and do not appear to be associated with an increase in mortality. About 50% of distant metastases are present at the time of diagnosis. Distant metastases occur even more often in patients with oncocytic carcinoma (35%) and in those patients who are >40 years at diagnosis. Sites of reported distant metastases: lung (49%), bone (25%), both lung and bone (15%), and the central nervous system or other soft tissues (10%).

timeline.title: After lobectomy for differentiated thyroid carcinoma
timeline.body: Physical examination, TSH measurement, and neck ultrasound at 6-12 months. Consider one-time Tg and TgAb measurement.

timeline.title: After total thyroidectomy without RAI
timeline.body: Physical examination, TSH, Tg and TgAb measurement at 6-12 weeks; neck ultrasound at 6-12 months.

timeline.title: After total thyroidectomy with RAI
timeline.body: Physical examination, neck ultrasound at 6-12 months, TSH, and Tg and TgAb measurement.

timeline.title: If NED (No Evidence of Disease)
timeline.body: Physical examination, TSH, annual Tg and TgAb measurement if stable, and neck ultrasound every 1-3 years for 5-8 years, then discontinue.

timeline.title: After 10-15 years of no recurrence (low risk)
timeline.body: No further monitoring for thyroid cancer indicated.

timeline.title: For medullary carcinoma
timeline.body: Postoperative calcitonin and CEA measurement 2-3 months postoperatively.

late_effects.name: Bone mineral density monitoring
late_effects.detail: Bone mineral density monitoring for patients on long-term TSH-suppressive levothyroxine therapy, particularly post-menopausal patients

late_effects.name: Cardiac monitoring
late_effects.detail: Cardiac monitoring for tachyarrhythmias in elderly patients on TSH-suppressive doses of levothyroxine

late_effects.name: Calcium and vitamin D supplementation counseling
late_effects.detail: Calcium and vitamin D supplementation counseling for patients with chronically suppressed TSH

late_effects.name: Salivary and lacrimal gland function monitoring
late_effects.detail: Salivary and lacrimal gland function monitoring after RAI therapy

late_effects.name: Screening for secondary malignancies
late_effects.detail: Screening for secondary malignancies in patients who received RAI therapy

late_effects.name: Screening for pheochromocytoma and hyperparathyroidism
late_effects.detail: Screening for pheochromocytoma (annual, MEN2B and MEN2A) and hyperparathyroidism (annual, MEN2A) in patients with hereditary MTC
